docs.title: Quickstart
docs.text: Django application that provides hashtag/tag functionality on top of django-taggit, with reusable, consumer-agnostic helpers to render and filter tags consistently across sites.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
These packages are required:

  • Python (3.9, 3.10, 3.11, 3.12, 3.13, 3.14)
  • Django (3.2, 4.2, 5.2)

We highly recommend and only officially support the latest patch release of each Python and Django series.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
1. Install using pip:

    pip install django-hashtag

  2. Add taggit and hashtag to your INSTALLED_APPS:

    settings.py
    INSTALLED_APPS = [
    # ...other apps
    "taggit",
    "hashtag",
]

  3. Run migrations:

    python manage.py migrate

docs.title: What you get
docs.text:
• A tag data model (MyTag, MyTagGroup, MyTaggedItem).
  • A reusable hashtag_chips template tag to render tags as themeable chips.
  • hashtag.filtering helpers to filter list views by the active tag.
  • An opt-in canonical tag URL via the HASHTAG_TAG_URL_NAME setting.

See the Templates guide for usage.

docs.title: Example
docs.text: • Check the demo repo on GitHub.

docs.title: Development
docs.text: See the Contribution guidelines for how to clone the repository, run the test suite and contribute changes back to django-hashtag.

docs.title: Clone
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/DLRSP/django-hashtag/
cd django-hashtag

docs.title: Run the test suite
docs.text:
pytest tests/ -v

Or across the supported matrix with tox:

tox -e py311-django42

docs.title: Pull requests
docs.text:
• Add tests for new behaviour.
  • Add a news/ fragment for any user-facing change (towncrier); for example news/+my-change.feature.rst.
  • Run pre-commit run --all-files before opening the pull request.

docs.title: References
docs.text:
• django-taggit — the tagging layer django-hashtag builds on.
  • Source code
  • Issue tracker
  • Changelog

docs.title: Third-party packages
docs.text:
django-hashtag builds on:

  • django-taggit — generic tagging for Django models.

docs.title: Example
docs.text: A quick walkthrough of using django-hashtag in a project.

docs.title: Run the demo project
docs.text:
git clone --depth=50 --branch=django-hashtag https://github.com/DLRSP/example.git DLRSP/example
cd DLRSP/example
python manage.py runserver

Then browse the app at http://127.0.0.1:8000.

docs.title: Tag a model
docs.text: django-hashtag builds on django-taggit. Attach a tag manager to your model and assign tags as usual, then render and filter them with the helpers documented in Templates.

docs.title: Images and assets
docs.text: django-hashtag renders text chips and does not require images. To match your site's look and feel, theme the chips with CSS custom properties on the hashtag-chip / hashtag-chip--<variant> classes (see Templates).

docs.title: Templates and helpers
docs.text: django-hashtag ships reusable, consumer-agnostic helpers so every site renders and filters tags the same way, without re-implementing markup or queryset logic.

docs.title: Render tags as chips: hashtag_chips
docs.text:
The hashtag_chips inclusion tag renders a tag collection as accessible chips. It accepts model instances (exposing name/slug/get_absolute_url) or plain strings.

{% load hashtag_tags %}

{# Static, non-linkable chips (e.g. a homepage badge row) #}
{% hashtag_chips tags variant="home" linkable=False %}

{# In-context filter links: builds <filter_url>?<filter_param>=<slug> #}
{% hashtag_chips tags variant="review" filter_url=request.path filter_param="tag" %}

{# Custom href pattern with a {slug} placeholder #}
{% hashtag_chips tags href_pattern="/reviews/?tag={slug}" %}

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text:
Argument Default Description tags — Iterable of tag instances or strings. variant "" Theme modifier; adds hashtag-chips--<variant> / hashtag-chip--<variant>. linkable True When False, render <span> chips instead of links. prefix "#" Text prefix shown before each tag name. filter_url "" Build an in-context filter link <filter_url>?<filter_param>=<slug>. filter_param "tag" Query parameter used with filter_url. href_pattern "" Link template with a {slug} placeholder. link_class "" Extra CSS class added to each link. channel "tag" Value for the data-channel attribute (analytics hook). placement "" Value for the data-placement attribute (analytics hook). label "" Accessible aria-label for the chip list.

Href resolution order: filter_url -> href_pattern -> get_absolute_url() (when reversible) -> none (renders a <span>).

docs.title: Theming
docs.text:
Style chips per site via CSS custom properties on the shared classes (hashtag-chip, hashtag-chip--<variant>). A base stylesheet ships at hashtag/static/hashtag/hashtag-chips.css. Load it (or your own theme) in your template:

{% load static %}
<link rel="stylesheet" href="{% static 'hashtag/hashtag-chips.css' %}">

docs.title: Filter a list by tag
docs.text:
hashtag.filtering shares the queryset filter and active-tag lookup used by in-context list views, without referencing any consumer model:

from hashtag.filtering import active_tag_slug, filter_queryset_by_tag

def review_list(request):
    slug = active_tag_slug(request)            # reads ?tag=<slug>
    reviews = filter_queryset_by_tag(Review.objects.all(), slug)
    # ...build your own context/template/pagination...

filter_queryset_by_tag accepts a lookup argument (default "tags__slug") for non-default relation paths; an empty slug returns the queryset unchanged.

docs.title: Optional canonical tag page: HASHTAG_TAG_URL_NAME
docs.text:
MyTag.get_absolute_url() is opt-in. A tag has a canonical page only when the project names the route via HASHTAG_TAG_URL_NAME:

settings.py
# Enable canonical per-tag URLs resolved with reverse(name, args=[slug]).
HASHTAG_TAG_URL_NAME = "tagged"

# Or disable canonical tag pages entirely (sites that surface tags only as an
# in-context ?tag= filter); get_absolute_url() then raises NoReverseMatch and
# hashtag_chips falls back to filter_url/href_pattern.
HASHTAG_TAG_URL_NAME = ""
